# KEYSTONE RECOMPOSITION — PRODUCTION BIBLE
<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / The Canonical Creative & Technical Reference — v1.0 -->
## The Canonical Creative & Technical Reference — v1.0
<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / Updated: 2026-05-22 -->
## Updated: 2026-05-22

> **PURPOSE**: This is the **single source of truth** for how all Keystone Recomposition video content is conceived, scripted, produced, published, and promoted. Every agent — Flash, Opus, overnight researchers, subagents, and Wayne himself — MUST consult this document before writing scripts, generating thumbnails, assembling DaVinci timelines, or publishing to any platform. It supersedes all prior creative notes.
>
> **Brands Covered**: Keystone Recomposition + Keystone Protocols (shared channel).
>
> **Owner**: Curtis "Wayne" Stevenson (`curtis4vancouver@gmail.com`)

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 1. THE DIGITAL TWIN STRATEGY -->
## 1. THE DIGITAL TWIN STRATEGY

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 1.1 Core Concept -->
### 1.1 Core Concept
Wayne runs an **AI Digital Twin avatar** (generated via HeyGen Avatar IV or equivalent) as the primary on-camera host for all Recomposition video content. This is a **long-term, indefinite commitment** — not a placeholder. The twin is the brand face.

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 1.2 Twin Maintenance Schedule -->
### 1.2 Twin Maintenance Schedule
- **Weekly Update**: Wayne re-captures his likeness (photo/video reference set) to keep the twin current with his real appearance — haircut, weight changes, tan, facial hair, aging, etc.
- **Seasonal Refresh**: Major avatar model retraining at least every quarter to prevent drift between the real Wayne and the digital Wayne.

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 1.3 Real Footage Integration -->
### 1.3 Real Footage Integration
The twin does NOT replace Wayne entirely. Real footage of Wayne is spliced in strategically to:
- **Prove authenticity**: Organic phone clips at the Squamish job site, gym, beach, trails.
- **Document milestone moments**: When Wayne starts doing retreats, building retreat facilities, traveling — he films himself doing those things and they get edited into the content.
- **Kill the "AI slop" algorithm penalty**: Platforms detect pure AI content. Real B-roll every 45-60 seconds defeats this.

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 1.4 Disclosure -->
### 1.4 Disclosure
Every video description includes the **AI Digital Twin / Synthetic Media Disclosure** at the bottom:
```
⚠️ SYNTHETIC MEDIA / AI DIGITAL TWIN DISCLOSURE: The host of this video is a 
photorealistic digital representation of Wayne Stevenson, synthesized using advanced 
visual networks. All real B-roll footage, audio journals, and personal health metrics 
are authentic and documented by the creator.
```

lve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/LICENSE|LICENSE]]|[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/LICENSE|LICENSE]]|[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/LICENSE|LICENSE]]|[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/starlette-1.0.0.dist-info/licenses/[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/LICENSE|LICENSE]]|[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/LICENSE|LICENSE]]|[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/LICENSE|LICENSE]]|[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/LICENSE|LICENSE]]|[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/starlette-1.0.0.dist-info/licenses/[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/starlette-1.0.0.dist-info/licenses/[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/LICENSE|LICENSE]]|[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/LICENSE|LICENSE]]|[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/LICENSE|LICENSE]]|[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/LICENSE|LICENSE]]|[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/starlette-1.0.0.dist-info/licenses/[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/LICENSE|LICENSE]]|[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/LICENSE|LICENSE]]|[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/LICENSE|LICENSE]]|[[davinci-resolve-mcp/venv/Lib/site-packages/uvicorn-0.46.0.dist-info/licenses/LICENSE|License]] #52603)** and construction superintendent is his **unfair E-E-A-T advantage** in the health/wellness space. No other peptide or GLP-1 creator has this angle.

**DO:**
- Reference the physical toll of decades of construction: "My shoulders carried 20 years of framing lumber."
- Mention general physical demands: "When you work with your body for a living, recovery isn't optional."
- Use construction metaphors: "Your body is a structure. Muscle is the load-bearing beam. Fat loss without muscle preservation is a structural failure."
- Frame peptide benefits through the lens of functional recovery: "My back recovery compressed from five days to forty-eight hours."
- Show organic B-roll on active job sites (hardhat, lumber, machinery, blueprints).

**DO NOT:**
- Talk about specific client projects, addresses, or contracts.
- Discuss Keystone Possibilities the business or its services.
- Blur the line between builder content and health content — the builder is the *person*, not the *topic*.
- Make it sound like construction workers should self-medicate.

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 2.2 The Character Arc -->
### 2.2 The Character Arc
The overarching series narrative is a **slow, documented personal transformation**:
- Wayne starts as a worn-down, overworked builder with chronic pain, weight gain, and declining energy.
- Each video documents one step of his methodical recovery protocol.
- Over weeks and months, he's visibly improving — stronger, leaner, sharper, sleeping better.
- This is a **serialized storyline**, not isolated topic videos. Each video references the previous one and teases the next.
- Future possibility: other "characters" (training partners, doctors, practitioners, supplement experts) may appear as the brand grows. For now, it's all Wayne.

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 3. CONTENT STRUCTURE RULES -->
## 3. CONTENT STRUCTURE RULES

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 3.1 Every Video MUST Have: -->
### 3.1 Every Video MUST Have:

| Order | Element | Requirement |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| **1st** | **Hook Question** | ALWAYS FIRST. First 3-5 seconds. A shocking stat, a provocative question, or a personal confession. Must stop the scroll. This is the FIRST thing the viewer hears — before anything else. |
| **2nd** | **Series Callback** | Immediately after the hook. 1 sentence referencing the previous episode: "Last week we covered X. Today we go deeper." For the FIRST video in the series, replace with a brief "Welcome to the series" intro. |
| **3rd** | **Into the Video** | After hook + callback, transition into the main content. The rest of the video flows from here. |
| **—** | **The Answer** | The video must answer a specific question the viewer is searching for. If someone Googles "does Ozempic cause muscle loss" and finds this video, they must get a clear, data-backed answer. |
| **—** | **Personal Case Study Framing** | Wayne doesn't prescribe. He documents. "Here's what I found in the published research, and here's what my personal logs show." |
| **—** | **Clinical Data References** | At least 2-3 published studies cited by name (trial name, journal, year). Shown on screen as text overlays. |
| **—** | **A Conclusion** | The viewer must leave with a clear takeaway — a framework, a protocol overview, or a next step. No cliffhangers without resolution. |
| **—** | **Medical Disclaimer** | In EVERY video, both spoken briefly and in the description. |
| **—** | **Music Channel Cross-Promotion** | Every video mentions the Keystone Recomposition music/training soundscape. "Link in the description for the official deep house training mix." |
| **—** | **Subscribe CTA** | End card or verbal CTA. For long-form: a visually cool animated subscribe button overlay. For shorts: verbal only. |

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 3.2 Series Continuity -->
### 3.2 Series Continuity
- Videos are **not standalone**. They form a connected series.
- Each video opens with a 1-sentence callback: "Last week we covered the muscle loss data. Today we're going deeper into the recovery stack."
- Each video closes with a tease: "Next Tuesday, we're breaking down the sleep protocol that changed everything."
- A master **Content Calendar** (tracked in `content_queue.json`) prevents topic overlap and ensures the narrative arc progresses logically.
- Agent Rule: **Before writing any new script, search the brain for all previously published scripts to avoid repeating topics, stats, or hooks.**

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 4. ASPECT RATIO FORMAT RULES (LOCKED — NON-NEGOTIABLE) -->
## 4. ASPECT RATIO FORMAT RULES (LOCKED — NON-NEGOTIABLE)

> ⚠️ **THIS IS LOCKED IN. DO NOT DEVIATE. DO NOT SUGGEST ALTERNATIVES.**

| Aspect Ratio | Format | Background | Platform | Production Tool |
|-------------|--------|------------|----------|----------------|
| **16:9** (Landscape) | **Long-Form** (8-10 min) | **Solid BLACK room** — no set, no scenery, no props. Wayne's face is the only anchor. | YouTube Long-Form | Google Flow / Omni — 10-second clips, 50 clips per 8:20 video |
| **9:16** (Portrait) | **Shorts** (15-60 sec) | **Dynamic real-world scenes** — job site, forest trail, gym, beach, urban walk. Wherever works for the topic. | YouTube Shorts, TikTok, IG Reels, FB Reels | Google Flow / Omni — composited avatar over dynamic backgrounds |

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / Why This Works: -->
### Why This Works:
- **16:9 black room** creates clinical authority. The viewer focuses 100% on Wayne and the data. Premium, stoic, no distractions. Omni handles this style flawlessly.
- **9:16 dynamic backgrounds** create high energy and scroll-stopping movement. Omni composites the avatar over real-world scenes exceptionally well. Scene-flip every 5-10 seconds keeps retention high.
- **Never mix these**. Long-form is ALWAYS black room. Shorts are ALWAYS dynamic backgrounds. This is the brand signature.

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 5. LONG-FORM VIDEO PRODUCTION STYLE (8-10 Minutes) -->
## 5. LONG-FORM VIDEO PRODUCTION STYLE (8-10 Minutes)

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 5.1 Visual Grammar -->
### 5.1 Visual Grammar

| Element | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| **Background** | Solid **black/very dark room** (the "block room"). Clean, clinical, premium. No set dressing. Wayne's face is the only visual anchor. |
| **Lighting** | Muted clinical teal/gray tones. Desaturated color grade. NOT bright, NOT warm, NOT friendly. This is stoic, authoritative, premium. |
| **Camera Moves** | Slow dolly push-ins for emphasis. Gentle lateral drifts. NO handheld shake. NO fast cuts during monologue. |
| **Zoom Dynamics** | Subtle zoom-ins on Wayne's face during key data reveals or emotional beats. Zoom-outs when transitioning topics. This keeps the viewer visually engaged on a static dark background. |
| **Hand Movements** | Wayne's avatar must gesture actively — pointing, counting on fingers, open palms. Hand movement is a critical retention driver on dark backgrounds. |
| **Facial Expression** | Wayne speaks with intensity, concern, conviction. NOT monotone. NOT overly excited. Stoic authority with occasional genuine emotion. |

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 5.2 B-Roll Overlay Pacing -->
### 5.2 B-Roll Overlay Pacing

**The 10-Second Rule**: Every 10 seconds of continuous face-talking, insert a **1-2 second B-roll overlay**. These overlays serve multiple purposes and rotate between:

| Overlay Type | Content | Frequency |
|-------------|---------|-----------|
| **Clinical Data Slides** | High-contrast screenshots of studies, charts, molecular diagrams. Yellow highlighting on key stats. | Every other overlay |
| **Organic Real B-Roll** | Wayne on the job site, at the gym, walking the beach, in the mountains. Phone-quality is fine — authenticity matters more than production value. | Every 3rd overlay |
| **Molecular/Cellular Animations** | Premium AI-generated visuals: peptide molecules flowing through bloodstream, cell signaling cascades (Akt-mTOR pathway), collagen triple helix formation, etc. | Every 4th overlay |
| **Music Channel Promo** | A sleek 1-2 second graphic: album art + "Training Mix ↓ Description" | Once per video |
| **Disclaimer Flash** | Brief text overlay: "Educational content only. Not medical advice. Consult your physician." | At least twice per video (once in first 2 minutes, once near end) |
| **Subscribe/Website CTA** | Animated button or text: "Subscribe for the next protocol drop" or website URL | End of video only |

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 5.3 The Ken Burns Effect on Stills -->
### 5.3 The Ken Burns Effect on Stills
When using high-end AI-generated still images (job site portraits, gym shots, beach photos, cellular/molecular renders):
- Apply a **slow Ken Burns zoom-in or zoom-out** (2-3% scale over 3-5 seconds).
- This creates movement from static images and keeps the viewer's eye engaged.
- Pair with the karaoke-style captions for maximum retention.

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 5.4 Karaoke-Style Captions -->
### 5.4 Karaoke-Style Captions
- All videos use **word-by-word animated captions** (karaoke style).
- Text appears in sync with each spoken word, highlighted as Wayne speaks.
- Font: Bold sans-serif (Inter Bold or similar). White text with a subtle dark shadow.
- Position: Lower third of the frame. Large enough to read on mobile.
- This is a proven retention technique across TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and now long-form content.

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 5.5 Color Grade -->
### 5.5 Color Grade
- **Desaturated clinical teal/gray/muted orange** palette.
- NO vibrant colors. NO warm Instagram filters.
- Think: medical documentary meets premium brand campaign.
- LUT: Custom or DaVinci Resolve built-in "Film Look" base → desaturate 30% → lift shadows to dark teal → compress highlights.

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 6. SHORTS PRODUCTION STYLE (15-60 Seconds) -->
## 6. SHORTS PRODUCTION STYLE (15-60 Seconds)

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 6.1 Visual Grammar — Different from Long-Form -->
### 6.1 Visual Grammar — Different from Long-Form

| Element | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| **Background** | NOT black room. Use **dynamic real-world backgrounds**: construction site, forest trail walk, gym, beach, urban walk. The avatar is composited over these backgrounds. HeyGen does an "awesome job" of this compositing. |
| **Avatar Overlay** | Wayne's digital twin speaking, layered on top of the moving background. Full upper body visible. |
| **Scene Flipping** | Shorts CAN and SHOULD flip between different background scenes every 5-10 seconds. Construction → gym → trail → beach. This keeps the visual energy high. |
| **Captions** | Same karaoke-style word-by-word captions as long-form. Critical for shorts — most viewers watch without sound. |
| **Pacing** | Fast. No slow dolly moves. Quick cuts. Higher energy than long-form but still stoic/authoritative — not "bro" energy. |

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 6.2 Shorts Content Strategy -->
### 6.2 Shorts Content Strategy
- Each long-form video generates **3-5 shorts** extracted from the most powerful segments.
- Shorts can also be standalone micro-topics that don't appear in long-form.
- Shorts feed into each other — viewer watches one, gets served the next. They should feel like a connected series even individually.
- Shorts always have a hook in the first 1 second.

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 6.3 Shorts Cross-Platform Publishing -->
### 6.3 Shorts Cross-Platform Publishing
All shorts publish simultaneously to:
- YouTube Shorts
- TikTok
- Instagram Reels
- Facebook Reels

(Via the social publishing pipeline documented in [[Master_Docs/20_SOCIAL_MEDIA_INFRASTRUCTURE_MASTER|20_SOCIAL_MEDIA_INFRASTRUCTURE_MASTER]].md](file:///c:/Users/Curtis/New%20folder/construction-website/Keystone_HQ/00_Master_Brain/Master_Docs/[[Master_Docs/20_SOCIAL_MEDIA_INFRASTRUCTURE_MASTER|20_SOCIAL_MEDIA_INFRASTRUCTURE_MASTER]].md))

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 7. PLATFORM COMPLIANCE & BAN PREVENTION -->
## 7. PLATFORM COMPLIANCE & BAN PREVENTION

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 7.1 Script Writing Rules (Anti-Ban) -->
### 7.1 Script Writing Rules (Anti-Ban)

Because peptides and GLP-1 content touch health/medical topics, scripts must be written to survive platform review on **all** platforms simultaneously:

| Rule | Reason |
|------|--------|
| **Never say "buy," "order," "source," or "get" followed by a peptide name** | Interpreted as promoting unregulated drug sales |
| **Never prescribe specific dosages** | Medical advice = ban trigger on TikTok/Meta |
| **Never claim to cure, treat, or prevent any disease** | FTC and platform health content policies |
| **Always frame as "published research shows" or "in my personal experience"** | Case study framing is compliant |
| **Never name grey-market vendors or websites** | Direct drug sourcing = immediate ban |
| **Use clinical terminology, not street names** | "Semaglutide" not "sema"; "BPC-157" not "BPC" |
| **Include "not medical advice" verbally at least once** | Spoken disclaimers carry more weight than text-only |
| **Never show injection procedures on TikTok** | TikTok specifically bans injection content |
| **Avoid "before and after" framing on Meta** | Instagram/Facebook flag B&A health transformation content |

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 7.2 Platform-Specific Adaptations -->
### 7.2 Platform-Specific Adaptations

| Platform | Adaptation |
|----------|------------|
| **YouTube (Long-form)** | Most permissive. Can discuss clinical data in depth. Include full disclaimer in description. |
| **YouTube Shorts** | Same as TikTok rules. Keep it educational, not promotional. |
| **TikTok** | Most restrictive. NO injection footage. NO dosing. NO "buy this." Frame everything as "I researched this" not "I did this." |
| **Instagram Reels** | Avoid B&A body transformation framing. Focus on education and storytelling. |
| **Facebook Reels** | Same as Instagram. Meta's health content policy applies. |

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 8. MUSIC INTEGRATION -->
## 8. MUSIC INTEGRATION

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 8.1 Core Philosophy -->
### 8.1 Core Philosophy
Music is **not background filler** — it is a **brand pillar**. Keystone Recomposition produces original deep house and ambient training soundscapes. Every video cross-promotes the music channel and playlists.

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 8.2 Integration Points -->
### 8.2 Integration Points

| Touchpoint | How |
|-----------|-----|
| **Video Background Audio** | Subtle deep house instrumentals ducked under Wayne's voice. Audible but not competing. Adds premium feel. |
| **Description Link** | Every video description includes: "🎵 Training Soundscapes: Listen to the official Keystone Recomposition Deep House Mix: [link]" |
| **Verbal Mention** | At least once per long-form video: "If you need a clean soundtrack for your training sessions, the link's in the description." |
| **B-Roll Overlay** | One 1-2 second graphic per video showing album art + "Training Mix ↓" |
| **Shorts Audio** | Shorts can use clips of the Recomposition music tracks as the background audio (TikTok-style). |
| **End Card** | Long-form end cards show the music channel link alongside the subscribe CTA. |

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 8.3 Music Channels & Assets -->
### 8.3 Music Channels & Assets
- **YouTube Music Playlist**: [Iron and Ice Deep House Mix](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LNlAiAu5YOo) (and future mixes)
- **Future**: Themed mixes per video series (e.g., "Cellular Pulse" for peptide content, "Recovery Session" for post-training)

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 9. PUBLISHING CADENCE -->
## 9. PUBLISHING CADENCE

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 9.1 Target Output -->
### 9.1 Target Output
| Format | Frequency | Day |
|--------|-----------|-----|
| **Long-Form (8-10 min)** | 2x per week | Tuesday + Thursday morning (5:30 AM PST) |
| **Shorts (15-60 sec)** | 3-5x per week | Derived from long-form + standalone. Publish daily if inventory allows. |

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 9.2 Content Pipeline -->
### 9.2 Content Pipeline
```
Research → Script → Voice Synthesis → Avatar Generation → B-Roll Assembly → 
DaVinci Edit → Caption Overlay → Thumbnail → Metadata → Publish
```

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 9.3 Anti-Overlap Protocol -->
### 9.3 Anti-Overlap Protocol
- Before writing ANY new script, the agent MUST:
  1. Search the brain: `search_master_brain("published scripts recomposition")`
  2. Check `content_queue.json` for upcoming scheduled topics
  3. Review the series arc to ensure the new video fits the narrative progression
- **If a topic has already been covered**, the agent must either find a fresh angle or propose a different topic.

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 10. HIGH-END STILL IMAGE GENERATION -->
## 10. HIGH-END STILL IMAGE GENERATION

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 10.1 Generation Tool: Google Flow + Reference Photos -->
### 10.1 Generation Tool: Google Flow + Reference Photos
All B-roll stills and motion clips are generated using **Google Flow** (Google's generative image/video tool). Every generation prompt must include a **reference photo of Wayne** to maintain visual consistency and likeness accuracy across all content. No stock photography. No third-party AI tools for B-roll.

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 10.2 Image Categories -->
### 10.2 Image Categories

| Category | Description | Google Flow Prompt Example |
|----------|------------|----------------------------|
| **Job Site Portraits** | Wayne in construction gear, hard hat, on a raw timber frame. Moody lighting. | "Man in hard hat and high-vis vest surveying a mountain home foundation at golden hour, Squamish BC, cinematic, desaturated teal grade" + reference photo |
| **Gym/Training** | Wayne performing heavy compound lifts. Clinical gym lighting. | "Athletic man mid-40s performing heavy T-bar row, dark gym, muted clinical lighting, visible muscle engagement" + reference photo |
| **Beach/Nature** | Wayne walking a windswept Pacific coast beach or through old-growth forest. | "Lone figure walking windswept Pacific coast beach, fog, desaturated teal, cinematic wide shot" + reference photo |
| **Cellular/Molecular** | Peptide molecules, cell signaling cascades, collagen fibers, GLP-1 receptor binding. | "GHK-Cu copper tripeptide binding to collagen triple helix, dark blue matrix, glowing white molecular structure, scientific visualization" (no reference photo needed) |
| **Clinical Data Viz** | Elegant charts, trial data, pathway diagrams. Custom generated. | "Clinical bar chart showing lean mass loss percentages, teal and gray palette, clean minimal design" (no reference photo needed) |

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 10.3 Visual Treatment -->
### 10.3 Visual Treatment
- Apply the Ken Burns effect (slow zoom in/out) on all stills in the timeline.
- Maintain the desaturated clinical color grade.
- Resolution: Minimum 1920x1080, prefer 4K for zoom headroom.
- **Consistency rule**: Wayne's appearance in generated images must match his current weekly reference photo. If the twin has been updated this week, use the latest reference.

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 11. RETREATS & FUTURE CONTENT EXPANSION -->
## 11. RETREATS & FUTURE CONTENT EXPANSION

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 11.1 Retreat Content -->
### 11.1 Retreat Content
When Wayne begins building or hosting wellness/longevity retreats:
- Film real footage of the build, the location scouting, the retreat experience.
- This becomes premium B-roll and standalone content.
- The digital twin can narrate over retreat footage.
- Retreat content bridges the builder persona (constructing the facility) with the wellness brand (hosting the experience).

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 11.2 Travel & Food Content -->
### 11.2 Travel & Food Content
- Travel and food retreat content is on the roadmap but not immediate priority.
- When activated, it integrates into the series arc naturally: "Building the infrastructure for my next chapter."

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 12. AGENT OPERATING RULES -->
## 12. AGENT OPERATING RULES

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 12.1 Before Writing Any Script -->
### 12.1 Before Writing Any Script
1. Read this document (21_RECOMPOSITION_PRODUCTION_BIBLE.md).
2. Read [[Master_Docs/20_SOCIAL_MEDIA_INFRASTRUCTURE_MASTER|20_SOCIAL_MEDIA_INFRASTRUCTURE_MASTER]].md](file:///c:/Users/Curtis/New%20folder/construction-website/Keystone_HQ/00_Master_Brain/Master_Docs/[[Master_Docs/20_SOCIAL_MEDIA_INFRASTRUCTURE_MASTER|20_SOCIAL_MEDIA_INFRASTRUCTURE_MASTER]].md) for [[.agents/rules/token_routing|token routing]].
3. Search the brain for previously published scripts to prevent overlap.
4. Check `content_queue.json` for the current publishing schedule.
5. Follow ALL compliance rules in Section 6.
6. Include ALL required elements from Section 3.1.
7. Apply the visual grammar from Section 4 (long-form) or Section 5 (shorts).

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 12.2 Script Output Format -->
### 12.2 Script Output Format
Every completed script package must include:
1. **Title Options** (3 choices, recommended first)
2. **Hook Line** (first 3-5 seconds, word-for-word)
3. **30-Tag Copy-Paste Matrix**
4. **10 Hashtags** (long-form set + shorts set)
5. **Full Description Template** (with timestamps, studies, disclaimers, music link)
6. **Thumbnail Concept** (text + visual direction)
7. **Full ElevenLabs V2 Phonetic Script** (with phonetic respellings of all medical/clinical terms)
8. **B-Roll Shot List** (timestamped: what overlay appears when)
9. **DaVinci Timeline Notes** (color grade, caption style, transition timing)
10. **Series Continuity Notes** (callback to previous video, tease for next video)

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 12.3 Content Calendar Tracking -->
### 12.3 Content Calendar Tracking
After any script is finalized and approved by Wayne:
1. Add it to `content_queue.json` with a target publish date.
2. Ingest the script metadata into the brain: `ingest_to_brain(content, source="published_script/{video_title}")`
3. Update this production bible if any creative [[DIRECTIVES|directives]] change.

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 13. PUBLISHED CONTENT ARCHIVE -->
## 13. PUBLISHED CONTENT ARCHIVE

> **PURPOSE**: Every published video is logged here so that new scripts can reference previous episodes, avoid repeating topics, and maintain the serialized storyline.

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 13.1 Published Videos -->
### 13.1 Published Videos

| # | Title | Topic | Key Stats/Claims | Callback From | Teases Next | File Reference |
|---|-------|-------|-----------------|---------------|------------|----------------|
| 001 | GLP-1 Muscle Loss: The Builder's Recomposition Protocol | GLP-1 muscle wasting, sarcopenia, 4-step protocol (protein, resistance, creatine, GHK-Cu) | STEP-1 trial 45% lean mass loss, SURMOUNT-1 25% lean mass, Keith Baar DEXA commentary | Series premiere — no callback | BPC-157 + TB-500 Wolverine Stack deep dive | `Research_Archives/SCRIPT_001_*` |

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 13.2 How to Use This Archive -->
### 13.2 How to Use This Archive
- **Before writing a new script**: Check this table to see what topics, stats, and hooks have already been used.
- **Series callbacks**: Open every video with a 1-sentence reference to the previous episode from this table.
- **Teases**: Close every video by teasing the next topic from this table.
- **Music references**: Every video must mention the training soundscapes. Check the Music Integration section.
- **Stat deduplication**: If a stat appears in a previous video, do NOT re-use it as a primary hook. Find a new angle.

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 13.3 Agent Rule -->
### 13.3 Agent Rule
After every video is approved by Wayne, the agent MUST:
1. Add a new row to the Published Videos table above
2. Ingest the script into the brain: `ingest_to_brain(content, source="published_script/{number}_{short_title}")`
3. Update `content_queue.json` with the next scheduled topic
4. Update `[[Master_Docs/ACTIVE_PROJECTS|ACTIVE_PROJECTS]].md` with the new current video status

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Recomposition Production Bible / 14. QUICK REFERENCE CHEAT SHEET -->
## 14. QUICK REFERENCE CHEAT SHEET

| Question | Answer |
|----------|--------|
| What's the avatar platform? | HeyGen Digital Twin (Avatar IV) / Google Flow Omni |
| How often does the twin update? | Weekly likeness refresh |
| **16:9 (long-form) background?** | **Solid BLACK room. ALWAYS. LOCKED IN.** |
| **9:16 (shorts) background?** | **Dynamic real-world scenes (job site, trail, gym, beach). ALWAYS. LOCKED IN.** |
| Long-form clip format? | 50 clips × 10 seconds = 8:20 via Google Flow/Omni |
| Shorts clip format? | Avatar composited over dynamic backgrounds via Omni |
| B-roll pacing? | 1-2 second overlay every 10 seconds of face-talking (added in DaVinci, NOT in clip engine) |
| Caption style? | Karaoke / word-by-word highlight |
| Still image treatment? | Ken Burns slow zoom + desaturated clinical grade |
| Color grade? | Desaturated teal/gray/muted orange. Clinical. Premium. |
| Music integration? | Every video. Description link + verbal mention + B-roll graphic |
| Publishing cadence? | 2 long-form/week (Tue/Thu 5:30 AM) + 3-5 shorts/week |
| Disclaimer required? | YES. Every single video. Spoken + written. |
| Can we prescribe dosages? | NO. Never. |
| Can we show injections? | NO on TikTok. Carefully on YouTube. |
| Can we name vendors? | NO. Never. |
| Persona? | Builder who's optimizing recovery — not a doctor, not a gym bro |
| Narrative arc? | Serialized case study — slow documented transformation |
| Cross-reference? | Check Section 13 Published Content Archive before every new script |
